zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Beaumont, Seigneur de Neubourg於大約1104年出生在英國瓦維其可爾時，他的父親Henry de Beaumont 1st Earl of Warwick59歲，母親Marguerite Perche26歲。他於1127年與Godechilde de Tosny在法國結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1159年8月30日享年56歲，埋葬在法國豪-諾門迪艾勒 貝-盧易。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Beaumont, Seigneur de Neubourg was born about 1104, in Warwickshire, England, his father, Henry de Beaumont 1st Earl of Warwick, was 59 and his mother, Marguerite Perche, was 26. He married Godechilde de Tosny in 1127, in France. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died on 30 August 1159, at the age of 56, and was buried in Le Bec-Hellouin, Eure, Upper Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Beaumont, Seigneur de Neubourg est né environ 1104, à Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry de Beaumont 1st Earl of Warwick, avait 59 ans et sa mère, Marguerite Perche, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Godechilde de Tosny en 1127, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé le 30 août 1159, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterré à Le Bec-Hellouin, Eure, Haute-Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Beaumont, Seigneur de Neubourg um 1104 in Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry de Beaumont 1st Earl of Warwick 59 und seine Mutter Marguerite Perche 26. Er hat 1127 in Frankreich Godechilde de Tosny geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb am 30. August 1159 im Alter von 56 Jahren und wurde in Le Bec-Hellouin, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Beaumont, Seigneur de Neubourg è nato il circa 1104 nel Warwickshire, England, il suo padre, Henry de Beaumont 1st Earl of Warwick, aveva 59 anni e il suo madre, Marguerite Perche, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Godechilde de Tosny nel 1127, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì il 30 agosto 1159, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolto a Le Bec-Hellouin, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Beaumont, Seigneur de Neubourgが約1104年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアで生まれた時，父Henry de Beaumont 1st Earl of Warwickは59歳，母Marguerite Percheは26歳であった。1127年にフランスでGodechilde de Tosnyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。彼は1159年8月30日に56歳で死去し，フランスハウテ-ノーマンディエウアル ベク-ヘロウインにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Beaumont, Seigneur de Neubourg(이)가 대략 1104년 영국 워윅실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry de Beaumont 1st Earl of Warwick(은)는 59세였고, 그의 어머니 Marguerite Perche(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1127년 프랑스에서 Godechilde de Tosny(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1159년 8월 30일 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 하테-노먼디 우르 일 베c-헬루인에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Beaumont, Seigneur de Neubourg nasceu aproximadamente 1104, em Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry de Beaumont 1st Earl of Warwick, tinha 59 anos e sua mãe, Marguerite Perche, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Godechilde de Tosny em 1127, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Ele faleceu em 30 de agosto de 1159, com 56 anos, e foi sepultado em Le Bec-Hellouin, Eure, Haute-Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Beaumont, Seigneur de Neubourg родился около 1104 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry de Beaumont 1st Earl of Warwick было 59 лет, а матери, которую звали Marguerite Perche, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Godechilde de Tosny в 1127 г. в местности Франция. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер 30 августа 1159 г. в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности л бек-хеллуин, эур, хауте-норманди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Beaumont, Seigneur de Neubourg nació aproximadamente 1104, en Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry de Beaumont 1st Earl of Warwick, tenía 59 años y su mamá, Marguerite Perche, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Godechilde de Tosny en 1127, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió el 30 de agosto de 1159, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultado en Le Bec-Hellouin, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia.
